features.summary: Broadcom reported strong Q2 fiscal 2021 results with consolidated revenue of $6.6 billion, up 15% year-over-year. Semiconductor solutions revenue grew 20% to $4.8 billion while infrastructure software revenue grew 4% to $1.8 billion. The company achieved record gross margins of 75% and strong free cash flow of $3.4 billion. Broadband and networking segments showed particular strength, with broadband growing 28% year-over-year driven by fiber PON deployments and Wi-Fi 6/6E demand, while networking grew 10% supported by hypercloud data center upgrades.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q3 consolidated revenue of $6.75 billion, representing 16% year-over-year growth. They anticipate continued double-digit growth in broadband through 2022 as service providers upgrade infrastructure for 5G competition. Networking is expected to maintain low double-digit growth with hypercloud customers upgrading to next-generation Tomahawk 4 platforms. The company sees gradual enterprise recovery but expects full normalization may not occur until 2022. Infrastructure software is projected to grow at better than mid-single digit rates.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints continue with extended lead times, though management notes these have stabilized. Cost inflation pressures exist across wafers, substrates and other components, though the company believes it can pass these through to customers. Enterprise demand recovery remains uncertain and could impact hypercloud spending patterns. There are concerns about potential handset inventory builds in China, though direct impact to Broadcom would be limited given its customer concentration in North America and Korea.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand from hypercloud customers upgrading to 400G and 800G networking platforms is driving networking growth. Service provider investments in 5G infrastructure and broadband upgrades (fiber PON, DOCSIS 4.0, Wi-Fi 6/6E) are creating sustained demand through 2022. Wireless content increases in FBAR and Wi-Fi technologies are supporting growth despite seasonal patterns. The company's strategic long-term customer agreements provide visibility and enable continued R&D investments in leading-edge technologies.
